description: John Zenon, a factory worker, presented to the occupational health clinic with acute severe contact dermatitis after exposure to industrial epoxy resins. As the occupational health specialist, document the incident in GNU Health by recording an active diagnosis using ICD-10 code L23 (Allergic contact dermatitis) or L24 (Irritant contact dermatitis) and adding an allergy or adverse reaction record for 'industrial resin'. Create a clinical evaluation documenting the acute presentation and prescribe a topical corticosteroid or oral antihistamine (such as Hydrocortisone, Loratadine, Cetirizine, Diphenhydramine, Betamethasone, or Prednisone). Schedule a follow-up appointment for between 7 and 14 days from today. Login with username 'admin' and password 'gnusolidario'.
